title: Hail!--And Farewell!
content:
They died that we might live,--
    Hail!--And Farewell!
    --All honour give
    To those who, nobly striving, nobly fell,
    That we might live!

    That we might live they died,--
    Hail!--And Farewell!
    --Their courage tried,
    By every mean device of treacherous hate,
    Like Kings they died.

    Eternal honour give,--
    Hail!--And Farewell!--
    --To those who died,
    In that full splendour of heroic pride,
    That we might live!
meaning:
William Arthur Dunkerley (John Oxenham)'s contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Hail!--And Farewell!"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
